Notes: Top level initial transition goes to Off.
Off state is explicitly defined.
Off transitions to On on powerOn which represents turning on.
On is modeled as a composite state.
On transitions to Off on powerOff which represents turning off.
Setup is modeled as a composite state inside On.
Setup initializes the crust region to Medium.
Defaults are initialized on powerOn and Setup starts with first course and timer not set.
Medium crust state is present.
Initial transition in crust selection enters Medium.
Medium transitions to Dark on crust.
Dark crust state is present.
Dark transitions to Light on crust.
Light crust state is present.
No explicit history state but orthogonal Setup regions preserve selections during menu and timer changes.
Menu is handled as a course-selection self transition without disturbing other setup choices.
Menu transition calls selectNextCourse().
Plus is handled within the TimerSetting region while staying in Setup.
Timer increases only below the 18 hour maximum and is ignored at 18 hours.
Plus actions increase the timer by 10 minutes.
Minus is handled within the TimerSetting region while staying in Setup.
Timer decreases only when at least 10 minutes are set.
Minus actions decrease the timer by 10 minutes.
Start from Setup enters Countdown when timer is greater than zero.
Delayed start transition has guard timer greater than zero.
Start from Setup enters BakingProcess when timer is zero.
Immediate baking transition has guard timer equals zero.
Countdown state is explicitly defined.
Course dependent times are represented through selectedCourse timing expressions.
Countdown transitions to BakingProcess when the timer elapses.
Stop during Countdown returns to Setup.
Direct baking uses selectedCourse timing expressions instead of a separate setup action.
BakingProcess is a composite state for the baking process.
BakingProcess has an initial transition.
Kneading state is present.
Kneading has entry action startKneading().
Kneading has exit action stopKneading().
Initial transition in BakingProcess enters Kneading.
Kneading transitions to Rising after the selected course kneading time.
Rising state is present.
Rising transitions to Baking after the selected course rising time.
Baking phase state is present inside the process.
Baking phase has do action heat().
Baking transitions to Warming after the selected course baking time.
Warming state is present.
Warming has do action warm().
Warming returns to Setup after 60 minutes.
Stop during BakingProcess returns to Setup.
Extra states for course and timer tracking are reasonable refinements.
Extra self transitions and reset transitions preserve intended behavior.
Extra guards handle timer boundaries consistently.
Extra actions such as clearTimer and countdown control are reasonable details.
Additional decomposition of Setup is a valid modeling choice.
Parallel regions reasonably model simultaneous setup choices.
No incorrect extra history states are present.
